findings.issue: Tasks 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 9 use the code-writing skill but omit dev-security-auditor from reviewers. The catalog default reviewer set for code-writing includes dev-security-auditor. The author deliberately scoped security review to the SQL/path-touching tasks (4, 5, 8), which is a reasonable curated choice. Note: Task 9 wires the throttle/first-tick state but the actual path/symlink handling now lives in Task 8 (which has dev-security-auditor), so Task 9's omission is consistent.
findings.fix: No action required. If a stricter policy is desired, add dev-security-auditor to all code-writing tasks; otherwise the current SQL/path-only scoping is acceptable.

findings.issue: internal/stat/fsstat.go appears in both 'Files to modify' and 'Files to read' of Task 8. Listing a file as both read-context and modify-target is redundant.
findings.fix: Drop fsstat.go from 'Files to read' in Task 8 since it is already a modify target (read access is implied). Confirm during decomposition that fsstat.go genuinely needs a new mount-prefix helper; if read-only, move it entirely to 'Files to read'.

summary: APPROVED. Round-2 re-validation confirms all round-1 fixes landed. (1) Waves are now file-disjoint: no two parallel tasks in any wave modify the same file. Wave 1 = Task1(pretty.go) / Task2(view.go,config.go,keybindings.go,verbose.go,help.go) / Task3(top/stat.go,stat_test.go) / Task4(query/common.go,stat/postgres.go); Wave 2 = Task5(query/common.go,overview.go,stat/postgres.go,stat/stat.go) / Task6(ui.go,layout.go,layout_test.go); Wave 3 = Task7(stat/stat.go); Wave 4 = Task8(top/stat.go,stat_test.go,fsstat.go); Wave 5 = Task9(stat/stat.go,top/stat.go); Final = Task10 QA. The prior Task 4/5 (common.go+postgres.go in Wave 1) and Task 9/10 (top/stat.go in Wave 3) conflicts are gone — Task 4 in W1, Task 5 in W2; Task 9 in W5, Task 10 is QA with no file modifications. (2) Cross-wave dependency ordering correct, no cycles: T5>T4, T6>T2, T7>T5, T8>T1/3/5/7, T9>T5/7/8; every dependency flows to a later wave. (3) Task descriptions are brief WHAT+WHY (each <=3 sentences; no embedded rationale with alternatives; no because/instead-of/rejected/we-chose markers; only purpose 'so'-clauses; (Decision N) are cross-references; no AC or TDD-anchor headings; no line-number references) — prior 'Task 5 over-detailed' and 'rationale in descriptions' MAJORs are cleared. (4) dev-security-auditor present on SQL + path tasks 4, 5, 8 as required. (5) All skills valid (code-writing / pre-deploy-qa); all reviewers (dev-code-reviewer, dev-security-auditor, dev-test-reviewer) are in the standard catalog and exist on disk. (6) All template sections present and non-empty: Solution, Architecture with both subsections, 10 Decisions each with Decision+Rationale+Alternatives, Data Models, Dependencies with both subsections, Testing Strategy (size L), Agent Verification Plan with all 3 subsections, Risks table, Acceptance Criteria, Implementation Tasks by waves, plus Backward Compatibility. (7) Final Wave has Task 10 Pre-deploy QA (pre-deploy-qa skill). (8) Task count 10 <= 15. (9) Per-task verification table rows 1-10 match the 10-task numbering and each task's Verify field; Task 10 row is `make build && make test && make lint`. (10) Frontmatter valid (created 2026-06-25, status draft, branch feature/010-overview-dashboard, size L); no time-based estimates; file paths consistent with architecture.md package layout (internal/query, internal/stat, internal/pretty, top/; new files overview.go/verbose.go/layout.go follow conventions). Two minor findings only (informational). Zero critical, zero major. Ready for task-decomposition.
